One of PVH’s greatest strengths is our people. Our collective desire is to create a workplace environment where every individual is valued, and every voice is heard, and we are committed to fostering an inclusive and diverse community of associates with a strong sense of belonging. Learn more about Inclusion \& Diversity at PVH **here**. *PVH Corp. or its subsidiary ("PVH") is an equal opportunity employer and considers all applicants for employment on the basis of their individual capabilities and qualifications without regard to race, ethnicity, color, sex, gender identity or expression, age, religion, national origin, citizenship status, sexual orientation, genetic information, physical or mental disability, military status or any other characteristic protected under federal, state or local law. In addition to complying with all applicable laws, PVH is also committed to ensuring that all current and future PVH associates are compensated solely on job\-related factors such as skill, ability, educational background, work quality, experience and potential.*

title: Functional and Solution Architect
description: As a leading financial services and healthcare technology company based on revenue, SS\&C is headquartered in Windsor, Connecticut, and has 27,000\+ employees in 35 countries. Some 20,000 financial services and healthcare organizations, from the world's largest companies to small and mid\-market firms, rely on SS\&C for expertise, scale, and technology. **Job Description** **Functional and Solution Architect** ------------------------------------- **Locations:** Dublin, Ireland \| Hybrid ### **Get To Know Us:** SS\&C is leading the way. We continue to look for todays and tomorrow’s brightest talent, those that embody a spirit to improve not only their lives, but those around them. From college students to seasoned and experienced professionals, we encourage you to apply. SS\&C prides itself on hiring diverse, honest, dynamic individuals, who value collaboration, accountability, and innovation to name a few. ### **Why You Will Love It Here!** * Flexibility: Hybrid Work Model * Your Future: Professional Development Reimbursement including access to SS\&C University * Work/Life Balance: Competitive holiday scheme * Your Wellbeing: Competitive benefits designed to support the wellbeing of our staff * Diversity \& Inclusion: Committed to Welcoming, Celebrating and Thriving on Diversity * Training: Hands\-On, Team\-Customised throughout your career ### **What You Will Get To Do:** * Act as the bridge between business requirements and technical delivery on a financial services wealth platform * Translate complex wealth management business needs into clear functional specifications and solution designs * Produce high\-quality documentation including functional specifications, solution design documents, process flows and data dictionaries * Facilitate requirements workshops and collaborate with stakeholders to define user stories, acceptance criteria and designs * Design integration approaches between platform components and third\-party services (e.g. custodians, data providers, payment rails) * Define data flows, entity relationships and reporting requirements across the platform * Collaborate with development, QA and architecture teams to ensure solutions are implementable, scalable and aligned with platform strategy ### **What You Will Bring:** * Deep analytical ability; capable of decomposing complex business processes into precise functional specifications * Strong communication and stakeholder management skills; able to present and defend designs to both business and technical audiences * Proactive in identifying opportunities to simplify, modernise and improve platform design * Collaborative approach; works effectively across business, development, QA and architecture teams * Commitment to continuous learning across both wealth domain knowledge and emerging technology patterns * 5\+ years’ experience in functional or solution architecture, business analysis, or a combined role within financial services * Deep knowledge of wealth management products and client journeys: investment portfolios, pensions, ISAs, savings products, suitability and appropriateness assessments, and client reporting * Proven ability to produce high\-quality functional specifications, solution design documents, process flows and data dictionaries * Experience facilitating requirements workshops and translating business needs into user stories, acceptance criteria and functional designs * Strong understanding of multi\-tier application architecture: front\-end portals, API layers, back\-end services and relational databases, sufficient to assess technical feasibility and impact * Experience designing integration patterns between wealth platform components and third\-party services * Knowledge of data modelling; ability to define data flows, entity relationships and reporting requirements * Familiarity with regulatory obligations in wealth management: client suitability, product governance, transaction reporting and audit trail requirements * Experience working in agile delivery environments; comfortable authoring epics, features and stories alongside product owners and development teams * Experience on a multi\-tenant wealth platform serving multiple client business units or brands from a single environment * Exposure to Angular\-based investor and advisor portal design from a functional perspective * Knowledge of open banking, API\-first platform design and RESTful service specifications * Familiarity with GDPR, data residency requirements and regulatory outsourcing obligations within EU financial services * Experience with ArchiMate, TOGAF or equivalent enterprise architecture frameworks * Exposure to AI\-assisted design tooling and the use of large language models to accelerate documentation and specification authoring * Experience using Jira for requirements traceability, story management and design review workflows; SharePoint for specification management and team collaboration We encourage applications from people of all backgrounds to enable us to bring diverse perspectives to our thinking and conversation. It's important to us that we strive to have a workforce that is diverse in the widest sense. Thank you for your interest in SS\&C! If applicable, to further explore this opportunity, please apply directly with us through our Careers page on our corporate website @ www.ssctech.com/careers . Unless explicitly requested or approached by SS\&C Technologies, Inc. or any of its affiliated companies, the company will not accept unsolicited resumes from headhunters, recruitment agencies, or fee\-based recruitment services. Applications will be accepted on an ongoing basis until the position is filled. SS\&C Technologies is an Equal Employment Opportunity employer and does not discriminate against any applicant for employment or employee on the basis of race, color, religious creed, gender, age, marital status, sexual orientation, national origin, disability, veteran status or any other classification protected by applicable discrimination laws.

title: German Customer Service and Sales Officer in Malta
description: Are you a fluent German speaker with a background in sales and a passion for customer excellence? Join a **Great Place to Work\-Certified** international bank that is redefining the "new generation" of digital lending. As a **Customer Service \& Sales Officer**, you will be a key driver in our German\-market operations. This role is designed for a target\-driven individual who can blend high\-level customer support with the ability to convert inquiries into successful loan and card applications. You’ll be working in a fast\-paced Fintech environment where your sales experience will directly impact the bank's growth. **Your Responsibilities*** **Sales \& Conversion:** Actively engage with German\-speaking leads to guide them through the loan application process and promote our card products. * **Customer Support:** Handle incoming inquiries via phone, email, and SMS, providing solutions that balance customer needs with business goals. * **Onboarding \& Verification:** Manage the customer journey from the first click to final verification, ensuring all documentation is accurate and compliant. * **Portfolio Growth:** Collaborate with the Payments and Collections teams to ensure customer retention and high satisfaction levels. * **Market Insight:** Use your sales expertise to identify customer pain points and suggest improvements to our product offerings. **Requirements** * **Language:** Native or C1 level fluency in **German** (essential) and a professional command of **English**. * **Sales Experience:** **Minimum 1–2 years of experience in a sales\-driven environment** (e.g., Telesales, Business Development, or Commission\-based Retail). * **Financial Literacy:** Previous experience in banking or financial services is highly preferred. * **Tech Savvy:** Strong proficiency in Microsoft Office, specifically **Excel**, and experience with CRM systems. * **Work Authorization:** You must hold an **EU Passport** or currently have the legal right to work in Malta. * **Attributes:** Resilient, persuasive, and motivated by hitting performance targets. **Benefits** * **Salary:** Competitive base salary of **€23,000 – €28,000 per year** (depending on experience). * **Bonuses:** Performance\-based bonus structure. * **Relocation Support:** Financial assistance (relocation bonus) to help you settle into life in Malta. * **Health \& Security:** Comprehensive Private Health Insurance and Life Insurance. * **Office Culture:** Modern office facilities featuring daily fresh fruit, coffee, and toast. * **Social Life:** Regular monthly after\-work events and "Casual Friday" drinks. * **Lifestyle:** Enjoy over 300 days of sunshine and a vibrant expat community in one of Europe's most popular island destinations.

title: Government Affairs/Public Policy Analyst
description: **✨ Public Affairs Associate – Economic Policy \& Research (Europe)** 📍 Dublin, Ireland (Hybrid – 2 days/week) \| IC Level: L3 **Client** : Leading Global Technology Company (household name in AI \& digital innovation) We’re hiring a **Public Affairs Associate** to support economic research and thought leadership that informs public policy across Europe. This role focuses on connecting digital trends (like AI) to economic impact and policy conversations. **🔹 What You’ll Do (Day\-to\-Day):** * Support and shape policy\-relevant research (topics like AI, labor markets, digital regulation) * Translate complex economic and tech insights into clear, policy\-friendly narratives * Coordinate with research partners, academics, and agencies on ongoing projects * Manage timelines, deliverables, and stakeholder inputs across multiple initiatives * Collaborate cross\-functionally with Public Policy, Comms, and Marketing teams * Contribute to reports, briefs, and thought leadership for policymakers **🔹 Non\-Negotiable Experience:** * Experience in **public affairs, policy research, think tanks, or government advisory** * Proven ability to **inform policy discussions** (not just implement policy) * Strong **stakeholder management** – influencing without authority in complex orgs * Ability to connect **technology → economic impact → policy implications** * Experience working across **multiple stakeholders, teams, or regions** **🔹 Bonus to Have:** * Exposure to **European policy landscape** * Background in **economics, political science, public policy, or international relations** * Experience working on **AI, digital economy, or regulatory topics** * International / cross\-cultural work experience

title: Graduate AI software engineer
description: At Bending Spoons, we’re striving to build one of the all\-time great companies. A company that serves a huge number of customers. A company where team members grow to their full potential. A company that functions at unparalleled levels of effectiveness and efficiency. A company that creates value for shareowners at an extraordinary rate. And a company that does so while adhering to high ethical standards. In pursuit of this objective, we acquire and improve digital businesses, not to sell on, but to own and operate for the long term. The transformations we make are often deep—designed to speed up innovation, benefit customers, and strengthen business performance. Here, hierarchy is minimal and teams are small and talent\-dense. We operate established products with the ambition, agility, and urgency of a startup. Across the company, we integrate AI deeply into how we work so that human judgment and machine intelligence reinforce each other. For a talented, driven, and collaborative individual, working at Bending Spoons is an opportunity to learn, make an impact, and progress their career at an exceptionally high rate. That’s our promise to such a candidate. **A few examples of your responsibilities** * **Build AI\-powered product experiences.** Work with product teams to identify high\-impact opportunities where AI can transform the user experience for millions—and then build these transformative solutions. Drive the process from possibility to production, delivering code that’s robust, reproducible, and built for scale. * **Amplify your impact with AI.** Integrate the most powerful AI tools directly into your development workflow—design, implementation, testing, and documentation—to move faster while maintaining high standards for correctness, reliability, and maintainability. * **Master your toolkit.** Work across diverse stacks with end\-to\-end ownership, choosing the right technologies for each challenge. From monoliths to microservices, gRPC to REST, Kubernetes to Docker, Python to Rust—you’ll apply technologies thoughtfully, focusing on depth and purpose rather than trends. * **Build software that matters.** Take real ownership from idea to production, creating systems used by millions and evolving them into products at scale. * **Simplify relentlessly.** Question every layer of complexity. Improve architectures, pipelines, and codebases to build systems that are simpler, more scalable, and easier to maintain. **What we look for** * **Reasoning ability.** Given the necessary knowledge, you can solve complex problems. You think from first principles, and structure your ideas sharply. You resist the influence of biases. You identify and take care of the details that matter. * **Drive.** You’re extremely ambitious in everything you do—and your initiative, effort, and tenacity match the intensity of your ambition. You feel deeply responsible for your work. You hold yourself to a high—and rising—bar. * **Team spirit.** You give generously and without the expectation of receiving in return. You support the best idea, not your idea. You're always happy to get your hands dirty to help your team. You’re reliable, honest, and transparent. * **Proficiency in English.** You read, write, and speak proficiently in English. **What we offer** * **Incredibly talented, entrepreneurial teams.** You’ll work in small, result\-oriented, autonomous teams alongside some of the brightest people in your field. * **An exceptional opportunity for growth.** We go to great lengths to hire individuals of outstanding potential—then, our priority is to put them in the ideal position to thrive. Spooners in their 20s lead products worth hundreds of millions of dollars. And if you’ve got what it takes, you’ll soon be playing an essential role in major projects, too. * **Competitive pay and access to equity in the company.** Typically, we offer individuals at the start of their career an annual salary of £85,797 in London and €66,065 elsewhere in Europe. For a candidate that we assess as possessing considerable relevant experience, the salary on offer tends to be between £112,189 and £250,512 in London, and €107,837 and €188,848 elsewhere in Europe. Compensation varies by location and expected impact, and grows rapidly as you gain experience and translate it into greater contributions. For individuals who demonstrate exceptional capability, we may offer compensation that extends beyond the usual ranges to reflect their higher expected impact. If you're offered a permanent contract, you'll also be able receive some of your pay in company equity at a discounted price, thus participating in the value creation we achieve together. If relocating to Italy, you may enjoy a 50% tax cut. * **All. These. Benefits.** Flexible hours, remote working, unlimited backing for learning and training, top\-of\-the\-market health insurance, a rich relocation package, generous parental support, and a yearly retreat to a stunning location. We help each Spooner set up the conditions to do their best work. * **A flexible start date and part\-time options.** You don't need to wait until graduation to apply. We offer flexible start dates and the possibility to begin part\-time, transitioning to full\-time as you complete your degree. Many Spooners joined before graduating and progressively took on greater responsibility, with arrangements that allowed them to do so without compromising their education. **Commitment \& contract** Permanent or fixed\-term. Full\-time. **Location** Milan (Italy), London (UK), Madrid (Spain), Warsaw (Poland) or remote in selected countries. **The selection process** In our screening process, we prioritize verifiable signals of excellence, regardless of seniority. Some people hold back because they feel they lack experience or have an “imperfect” CV. If you like the role and believe you could excel over time, don’t self\-reject. If you pass our screening, you’ll be asked to complete one or more tests. They are challenging, may involve unfamiliar problems, and can take several hours. We set the bar high and won’t extend an offer until we’re confident we’ve found the right candidate. This is why a job may remain open for months or be reposted several times. We consider all applicants for employment and provide reasonable accommodations for individuals with disabilities—please let us know through this form. **Before you apply** If you’ve applied before but didn't receive an offer, we recommend waiting at least one year before applying again. Bending Spoons is a demanding environment. We’re extremely ambitious and we hold ourselves—and one another—to a high standard. While this tends to lead to extraordinary learning, achievement, and career growth, it also requires significant commitment. To help you ramp up quickly and set yourself up for success, we recommend spending your first few months working from our Milan office, regardless of your long\-term work location. It’s the best way to rapidly absorb our company culture and build trust with your new teammates. We’ll support you with generous travel and accommodation assistance. After that, you’re welcome to work from our offices in Milan or London, or remotely from approved countries—depending on what we agree at the offer stage. If the role speaks to you and you’re excited to give your best, we’d love to hear from you. Apply now—we can’t wait to meet you.

title: Graduate Program
description: **Launch Your Global Career After Graduation** **Teach English in China \| Full\-Time \| Entry\-Level Friendly** Just graduated (or graduating soon) and not ready for a desk job? Want to travel, gain international work experience, and build transferable skills while earning a competitive salary? **English 1** is hiring **recent graduates and early\-career professionals** to teach English in China. No prior teaching experience required. Full training, visa sponsorship, and structured career development are included. This is an ideal **first job** , **graduate program alternative** , or **gap\-year career launch** for ambitious, globally minded graduates. **What you'll do** * Teach English to young learners aged 3–16 in a fun, interactive, and immersive classroom setting. * Deliver high\-energy lessons using English 1’s award\-winning curriculum and digital tools. * Provide constructive feedback and help students build confidence in their English skills. * Take part in school events, cultural activities, and student engagement programs. **What you’ll get** * Competitive salary \+ performance bonus after 6 months * 21 days paid leave (10 days paid leave \+ 11 days statutory holidays) * Flight allowance * International health insurance (co\-paid) * Airport pick\-up support * \*\*Sponsored legal Z work visa \+ up to 2 weeks sponsored hotel stay * Comprehensive induction training, ongoing support, and fully prepared teaching materials * TEFL certificate \+ skill development \& career advancement * Distance learning courses (Level 6 Trinity College CertPT, DELTM) * English 1\-organized social and cultural activities \+ free Mandarin Chinese lessons *The hotel accommodation for the first two weeks will be deemed as a sponsorship upon successful completion of the first\-year contract. \*\*Sponsored legal Z work visa expenses will be reimbursed.* **Who we’re looking for** * A bachelor’s degree (in any subject) * A valid passport * A genuine interest in education, travel, and cultural exchange. * ESL certification (TEFL, TESOL, CELTA) is a plus\- we can sponsor * Clean background check. **Why work at English 1?** * Teachers are at the core of everything we do at English 1 * Over **30 years** of success in China with **200\+ schools** in **50\+ cities** . * Strong presence in **China** and **Indonesia** . * **Professional development** and career **advancement opportunities** . Whether you’re a recent graduate, or exploring your first international role, this is your chance to **earn, travel, and grow professionally and personally** . **Apply now and start your international career with English 1\.** \#EntryLevel \#Graduates \#EnglishTeacher \#ESL \#TEFL \#TeachingJobs \#GraduateJobs \#hiring \#jobs \#entrylevel \#teachercns \#graduates \#esl \#tefl \#teach \#JobAlert \#NowHiring \#JobOpening \#CareerOpportunities \#JobSearch \#WorkWithUs \#LinkedInJobs \#JobSeeker \#OpenToWork \#CareerGoals \#JobHunt \#NewJob \#CollegeGraduates \#BachelorsDegree \#RecentGrad \#ClassOf2026 \#JobSearch \#CareerSuccess \#EntryLevelJobs \#HiringNow \#YoungProfessionals \#CareerGrowth \#Networking \#LinkedInNetworking \#FutureLeaders \#UniversityAlumni \#HigherEd \#AlumniNetwork \#CollegeSuccess

title: Graduate Sales Executive (Nordics Market)
description: **About Revolut** People deserve more from their money. More visibility, more control, and more freedom. Since 2015, Revolut has been on a mission to deliver just that. Our powerhouse of products — including spending, saving, investing, exchanging, travelling, and more — help our 70\+ million customers get more from their money every day. As we continue our lightning\-fast growth,‌ 2 things are essential to our success: our people and our culture. In recognition of our outstanding employee experience, we've been certified as a Great Place to Work™. So far, we have 13,000\+ people working around the world, from our offices and remotely, to help us achieve our mission. And we're looking for more brilliant people. People who love building great products, redefining success, and turning the complexity of a chaotic world into the simplicity of a beautiful solution. **About The Role** Our Sales team powers customer growth around the world. From prospecting to acquisition and activation, they own their market segments, solve complex problems, and help more customers discover the value of Revolut with a sharp, solution\-oriented approach. We're looking for a Sales Executive to join our Graduate Sales Programme, which offers an unparalleled opportunity for ambitious graduates, regardless of prior experience, to join a high\-performing, international team. You'll be at the forefront of delivering cutting\-edge financial solutions to customers while building key relationships. You’ll kick things off with a focused bootcamp, complete with workshops, lectures, and hands\-on training to sharpen your commercial and communication skills. Once you're up to speed, you'll dive straight into the on\-the\-job experience by working with real clients. Watch this video from our team members about what goes into being an Account Executive at Revolut. Up to shape what's next in finance? Let's get in touch. **What You'll Be Doing** * Outreaching prospects across multiple touch points and channels, with an emphasis on cold\-calling * Developing and executing a robust lead\-sourcing strategy by identifying key verticals, diversifying your prospects base, and maintaining consistent activity levels * Building and managing your own pipeline, from prospecting to closing and account management, maximising adoption and upsell/cross\-sell opportunities * Liaising customer relationships with internal stakeholders to ensure a smooth experience, from getting started to daily usage * Being autonomous to effectively balance your time (prospect vs. close vs. upsell), activate internal resources, and engage your team * Using solution\-based selling to understand customer needs and communicate the value proposition * Becoming a product expert on Revolut Business, its market positioning, and the solutions it offers, backed by relevant, compelling case studies **What You'll Need** * Fluency in English * Fluency in Danish, Finnish, Norwegian, or Swedish * To have graduated from university in 2024 or 2025 * Excellent communication skills and articulation over the phone * Impeccable literacy skills (email, LinkedIn outreach) * A drive to launch a career in a high\-performing sales culture * An interest in the financial industry **Nice to have** * Previous internship or experience in a high\-performance, fast\-paced environment, with exposure to strategic thinking, analytical tasks, or client\-facing work Building a global financial super app isn’t enough. Our Revoluters are a priority, and that’s why in 2021 we launched our inaugural D\&I Framework, designed to help us thrive and grow everyday. We're not just doing this because it's the right thing to do. We’re doing it because we know that seeking out diverse talent and creating an inclusive workplace is the way to create exceptional, innovative products and services for our customers. That’s why we encourage applications from people with diverse backgrounds and experiences to join this multicultural, hard\-working team. **Important notice for candidates:** Job scams are on the rise. Please keep these guidelines in mind when applying for any open roles. * Only apply through official Revolut channels. We don’t use any third\-party services or platforms for our recruitment. * Always double\-check the emails you receive. Make sure all communications are being done through official Revolut emails, with an @revolut.com domain. **We won't ask for payment or personal financial information during the hiring process.** If anyone does ask you for this, it’s a scam. Report it immediately. By submitting this application, I confirm that all the information given by me in this application for employment and any additional documents attached hereto are true to the best of my knowledge and that I have not wilfully suppressed any material fact. I confirm I have disclosed if applicable any previous employment with Revolut. I accept that if any of the information given by me in this application is in any way false or incorrect, my application may be rejected, any offer of employment may be withdrawn or my employment with Revolut may be terminated summarily or I may be dismissed. By submitting this application, I agree that my personal data will be processed in accordance with Revolut's Candidate Privacy Notice

To help you ramp up quickly and set yourself up for success, we recommend spending your first few months working from our Milan office, regardless of your long\-term work location. It’s the best way to rapidly absorb our company culture and build trust with your new teammates. We’ll support you with generous travel and accommodation assistance. After that, you’re welcome to work from our offices in Milan or London, or remotely from approved countries—depending on what we agree at the offer stage. If the role speaks to you and you’re excited to give your best, we’d love to hear from you. Apply now—we can’t wait to meet you.

title: Group Financial Accountant
description: Join Keelings! You will be part of a passionate team of people and a growing business. Come on the journey with us where you will grow and develop and truly feel a sense of belonging. At Keelings, we don't just grow quality fresh produce, we also nurture people and their professional development. The Keelings family always employ the best people for the job, the key to our success is the quality of the people we employ. **Purpose Of The Role** This role is within the Group Finance function. The successful candidate will play a key role within the areas of Group reporting, assisting in the management of annual audit, internal governance reviews and ensuring compliance with Group Policy \& procedures as well as a wide range of other responsibilities. This is an opportunity for an enthusiastic and ambitious qualified accountant to learn and get exposure to many financial aspects of a large fast\-moving business. **Key Accountabilities \& Responsibilities** Duties will include some of the following tasks but may be subject to change: * Assisting with management of the annual external audit * Assisting with preparation of the monthly consolidation * Assisting in conducting balance sheet reviews of all group operating companies once per quarter * and summarising findings back to CFO as well as Audit, Risk \& Finance Committee * Conducting detailed control reviews as directed by CFO / Group Financial Controller * Responsible for updating of Policies and procedures across the Group as directed by CFO and Group Financial Controller * Responsibility for identification of eligible grants and assisting operating Finance teams in the successful completion of claims and were necessary, co\-ordinate the assistance of external specialists to assist in the process * Responsible for completion of all reporting to Agri Food Regulator as well as Enterprise Ireland * Approving weekly supplier and payroll payments * Approving FX trades on daily basis * Ad hoc projects and assisting with systems implementations and upgrades **Essential** **Key Skills \& Knowledge** * To have an open mindset and a willingness to grasp responsibility as offered and deliver on tasks assigned particularly when these are challenging and new * Practice background with experience of audit and financial statements a necessity * Comfortable interacting with senior management and other functional areas * Good communication \& presentation skills **Desirable** * Previous experience of governance / internal audit would be a distinct advantage * Ability to work on own initiative and as well as part of team * Ability to manage time and work to strict deadlines * Attention to detail and a desire to complete work to a high standard is a prerequisite * Excellent IT skills required, experience of RPA and/or VBA an advantage but not required * Experience of SUN / Infor Accounting system an advantage * Ability to speak French, Portuguese or Spanish an advantage but not required. * FMCG experience preferred. **Keelings Values** * People Matter \- We support and treat each other in a clear, fair and respectful way. We all learn, develop and encourage everyone to contribute * Teamwork \- We inspire and help each other to deliver our ambitions together as One Keelings * Integrity \- We do the right thing and deliver on our commitments * Passion for Achievement \- We motivate ourselves and each other to achieve our ambitions with energy, knowledge and courage. We are always striving to be better. **Recruitment Process** * CV Submission \& email acknowledgment of receipt * Phone Screening * 1st Interview * 2nd Interview * Outcome \& Feedback **Inclusion \& Diversity Statement** In Keelings we value all perspectives and knowledge that our colleagues bring to our business. We are committed to forming a diverse and inclusive team where all can share their unique experiences and perspectives. We welcome applications from all cultures and backgrounds and are dedicated to forming an environment of inclusiveness and diversity where all can thoroughly feel a sense of belonging. **Reasonable Accommodation Statement** If you require reasonable accommodation in submitting your application, interviewing, completing any pre\-employment testing, or otherwise participating in the recruitment process, please contact recruitment\_keelings@keelings.com Keelings Careers \- https://www.keelings.com/careers\-keelings/ Keelings Strategy \& Values – https://www.keelings.com/our\-people

title: HR Business Partner, PXT Ireland, PXT Ireland
description: **DESCRIPTION** --------------- In pursuit of being Earth's Best Employer, our global Human Resources teams, known as People eXperience and Technology (PXT), drive business success by fostering a safe, inclusive, and innovative culture. The HR Business Partner is responsible for operational excellence and organizational health at assigned site(s), serving as an individual contributor partnering directly with Field Leaders and site leadership teams. You will strengthen culture and deliver business results by translating organizational priorities into actionable plans, executing business goals through data\-driven decision making, and partnering with campus leaders to improve the employee experience. This role is accountable for the end\-to\-end employee experience, owning site\-level feedback mechanisms, recognition programs, and environment standards that shape trust, engagement, and performance. You will be expected to work onsite at an Amazon facility, supporting multiple sites within your region (campus) with regular mobility across assigned locations. Level 5 HR Business Partner Job Description Template I Global Operations PXT Last Updated: February 2, 2026 Basic Qualifications: \[EU/ROW – May modify degree types based on relevancy and local language requirements. Do not modify minimum year requirements] Key job responsibilities Employee Experience \& Engagement * Foster two\-way communication between employees and leaders through feedback mechanisms like Voice of the Associate, roundtables, and GEMBA walks * Execute and monitor employee engagement strategies and recognition programs, including budget planning and employee appreciation activities * Build an environment where employees feel safe and included, establishing Employee Resource Groups and ensuring equitable talent practices * Maintain meaningful floor presence across the site, gathering insights and identifying early signs of disengagement Operational Excellence \& Site Standards * Conduct regular site walks to assess workplace conditions and maintain facility standards in alignment with Amazon standards * Partner with site leaders and safety teams to ensure consistent execution of safety programs * Lead community initiatives at your site(s), partnering across your node and with Public Policy and Public Relations teams * Improve site effectiveness by identifying systemic patterns, addressing root causes, and ensuring consistent, fair application of policies Data\-Driven Decision Making \& Technology * Use data analytics to identify emerging trends, predict challenges, and implement proactive solutions * Drive adoption of HR technology solutions, AI, and self\-service tools while maintaining high\-touch service delivery in critical moments Strategic PXT Leadership * Partner with site leadership to balance operational needs with Amazon's values and people priorities * Coach Business Leaders on effective people management practices, including performance feedback, team development, and employee relations matters * Support change implementation by reinforcing messaging, addressing resistance, and helping leaders translate change into local routines * Execute core PXT processes while building trust and influence through sound business acumen A day in the life Your day will be a dynamic blend of strategic thinking and data\-driven decision\-making. You'll leverage advanced analytics and AI\- enabled tools to identify trends, anticipate business needs, and develop solutions that enhance workplace experience. From coaching leadership teams on workforce management to driving HR process excellence, you'll collaborate with Field leaders on operational objectives and team effectiveness. You'll maintain strong floor presence, building partnerships across all levels while contributing to meaningful workplace improvements through site walks, employee feedback sessions, and strategic initiatives. Success comes from your ability to navigate organizational dynamics, lead change initiatives, and make high\-judgment decisions in ambiguous situations. Through business acumen, data\-driven insights, and strategic partnerships, you'll help create a workplace culture where employees feel valued and empowered.**BASIC QUALIFICATIONS** ------------------------ * Knowledge of local labor law * Experience in HR within an international, fast\-paced and customer\-driven environment * Experience with MS Office tools and HR Information Systems * Bachelor's degree or equivalent **PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS** ---------------------------- * Master's degree, or MBA * Experience within a distribution center, logistics, or manufacturing environment * Experience partnering with senior leaders to deliver results * Experience with employee relations * Ability to effectively train, guide, and support others in the use of various HR technologies and digital platforms, with a focus on * increasing user adoption and enhancing end\-user experience * Coaching and influencing skills with ability to guide leaders through complex situations * Experience supporting employee engagement and development initiatives with measurable impacts on employee retention or satisfaction Amazon is an equal opportunities employer. We believe passionately that employing a diverse workforce is central to our success. We make recruiting decisions based on your experience and skills. We value your passion to discover, invent, simplify and build. Protecting your privacy and the security of your data is a longstanding top priority for Amazon. Please consult our Privacy Notice (https://www.amazon.jobs/en/privacy\_page) to know more about how we collect, use and transfer the personal data of our candidates. Amazon is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of protected veteran status, disability, or other legally protected status. Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please visit https://amazon.jobs/content/en/how\-we\-hire/accommodations for more information. If the country/region you’re applying in isn’t listed, please contact your Recruiting Partner.

title: Health And Safety Officer
description: **Health and Safety Officer** **Health and Safety Officer** with 4 years\+ experience required for a top tier main building contractor to work on a the tallest building in Ireland which will be built in Dublin City Centre. The successful Health and Safety Officer will ideally have worked in a similar position in Ireland previously for a Main Building Contractor. **Requirements:** * Hold a relevant Health and Safety Qualification * Construction background * Have a minimum of 4 years’ experience as safety officer on building projects in Ireland or the UK * Hold a full, clean Driving license * Excellent IT skills with proficiency in Microsoft Office including strong excel skills * Exhibit strong interpersonal and communication skills * Highly organised * Strong leader and ability to manage several projects at one time. **Package** * €65k\-€75k * Pension and other benefits * Excellent career prospects * Company vehicle / car allowance If you are a **Health and Safety Officer** considering a career move, please feel to send your **CV or contact Declan Hasson for a confidential discussion.** GPC will not send any applicants CV’s to clients without a full discussion and prior consent. GPC is a specialist construction recruitment consultancy based in Dublin and has more than 28 years’ experience working in the recruitment of construction professionals within Ireland and Internationally.

title: Human Resources Associate, Consulting
description: Trindent Consulting is a global management consulting firm that specializes in technical augmentation in the energy sector, specifically downstream Oil \& Gas. We’ve helped over 100 clients achieve significant financial benefits that yield between 500\-1500% return\-on\-investment within the 1st year – and we’ve done that without capital investments or software changes of any kind. Established in 2008, Trindent Consulting has quickly garnered a reputation as a firm that Makes It Happen™. We are ranked as one of Canada’s Fastest\-Growing Companies by Canadian Business and GROWTH 500 for 7 years in a row and named one of the Fastest\-Growing Consulting Firms by Consulting Magazine in 2015 through 2020\. Our Corporate Services team based in our Toronto Head Office manages our finance, human resources, back office, administration and IT support services for our global employees, customers and stakeholders. This position is based 5 days a week in our Dublin office. **Role Overview** The Human Resources Associate will manage the HR Administrative functions for a growing professional services firm headquartered in downtown Toronto, providing accountability for flawless execution and superior internal client service. **Responsibilities** * Plan, Schedule and Manage the onboarding of new staff members. * Display role\-model business acumen and communicate Company tasks, assignments, policies and procedures with clarity. * Assist in scheduling recruitment interviews and testing. * Prepare recruitment reporting documents and status updates for managerial review. * Management of accurate and fulsome data and documentation in the firm’s HRIS. * Oversight of the company vacation, sick day, flex time and bereavement day tracking. * Prepare business visa and coordinate any work permitting documentation with relevant authorities. * Assist in workplace investigations to ensure compliance with policies, laws and regulations. * Oversee the company drug testing compliance program and ensure that staff are adequately credentialed for the work they perform. * Help to coordinate the bi\-annual performance evaluation process. * Coordination of ‘value excellence’ programs to reward employees for their contributions to the success of the firm. * Perform other duties and ad hoc projects as assigned. **Education, Experience, and Skills Required** * Bachelor’s degree in Human Resources Management * 2\-3\+ years of HR experience and working towards CIPD designation * Experience of Irish \&/or European HR is preferred * Exceptional written and verbal communication skills. * Proficient user of Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, Power Point and Outlook). * Experience using a Bamboo HR HRIS suite is an asset. * Experienced and proficient in using a variety of telecommunication platforms (i.e., conference calls, video conferencing, web meetings, etc.) * Works with a sense of urgency. * Detail\-oriented and disciplined in self\-review. * Ability to manage competing demands and priorities. * Assertive and confident to work with strong personalities. * Embraces problem solving challenges and opportunities. * Proven experience in leading HR initiatives. * Experience working in diverse cultural and geographical settings. * Demonstrated ability to drive best practices and innovation in the HR domain. This role will see you work as part of a Global team for a fast growing consulting practice that offers depth of work and scope as we meet the demands of our expanding customer base and team. Our HR function enables our business and staff so we can deliver impact and results. *Trindent Consulting is committed to providing an accessible, respectful and inclusive environment for all employees and job applicants. Environment. * Develop and implement industry standard best practice infrastructure designs to ensure scalability, resilience, and security of infrastructure services to the daa business. * Vulnerability \& Security patch management * Produce high\-quality documentation to support Internal and external audits * Complete daily, weekly and/or quarterly System Administration tasks as and when necessary to ensure that the environment remains in a healthy state. * Using industry and company best practice and information, provide improvement and efficiency suggestions to existing team procedures and processes on an ongoing basis * Using industry \& corporate best practices \& standards, design and build complex Microsoft related infrastructure environments with an emphasis on security hardening * Implement high\-availability and disaster\-recovery strategies for critical applications. * Collaborate with application teams to ensure performance, resilience, and secure traffic flows. * Resolve technical issues upon escalation from first and second level support. * Mentor first and second line staff to assist their development and help them contribute to team deliverables. * Provide Infrastructure and security performance monitoring – to ensure critical components are monitored to meet our SLA’s. * Continuous development of plans for failover and testing of critical operational components and services to ensure resilience is in place. **Experience** * More than 5 years’ Senior experience in Microsoft technologies working in a large enterprise environment * *MCSA, MCP and/or CCNA certified* * Excellent Windows Server 2019/2022/2025 implementation \& administration skills * Expert knowledge of Active Directory and Group Policy * Excellent knowledge of network protocols and concepts * Excellent knowledge of backup solutions and principles, NetBackup experience a bonus * Excellent Windows client OS skills – Windows 11\. * Virtual platform (VMware) and storage technology experience (not essential but desirable) * Linux (RedHat/OEL) experience is desirable (not essential but desirable) * Proven analytical and problem resolution skills and methodologies * Ability to work on own initiative and work well within a team * Good team player with good communication skills at all levels of seniority * Good organizational, administrative \& documentation skills * Proven experience is working under strict process control methodologies, e.g. ITIL * Proficiency with automation tools (Python, Ansible, REST APIs). (not essential but desirable)

title: IT Systems Administrator
description: ### **Overview** This is a technical role within the Enterprise Services team reporting to the I.T. Service Desk Team Lead. You will work with the system administration and managed service partner to support the operation, performance, and availability of on\-premises and cloud infrastructure and core services. The role will focus on ensuring stable service delivery across the hybrid environment and enterprise platforms, with responsibility for day\-to\-day operational support, incident management, and continual service improvement. As required, you may also contribute to and act as a technical stakeholder in I.T. initiatives and projects. ### **Experience** * Proven experience as a system administrator supporting server operating systems (Windows Server 2019 above). * Experience supporting infrastructure and workloads in cloud environments (Azure) is essential. * Strong experience in managing Active Directory (on\-premises) and Microsoft Entra\-ID (Azure), including identity lifecycle management, role\-based access control (RBAC), and Group Policy. * Proficiency in administering M365 services, to include Exchange Online, Teams, SharePoint, OneDrive, and Intune. * Experience working with monitoring tools and performance management of infrastructure and services. * Exposure to security tools and technologies (patch management, antivirus, firewalls) an advantage. ### **Role Responsibilities** ### **System Administration** * Daily administration and support of IT infrastructure including servers, virtual environments, and cloud platforms. * Monitor system performance and availability, proactively identifying and resolving issues impacting service delivery. * Review system health reports, troubleshoot issues, and coordinate resolution with internal teams and external partners. * Ensure optimal performance, capacity planning, and reliability of infrastructure services. * Assist with deployment and lifecycle management of Windows operating systems, applications, and updates using Microsoft tools (Autopilot, Intune). ### **Identity and Access Management (Active Directory / Entra)** * Administer Active Directory and Entra ID environments across hybrid infrastructure. * Manage user identities, access provisioning, and deprovisioning processes. * Implement and maintain role\-based access control (RBAC) aligned with business requirements. * Maintain and optimise Group Policy and identity\-related configurations. ### **M365 Administration** * Administer M365 services including Exchange Online, Teams, SharePoint, and OneDrive. * Monitor service health, troubleshoot issues, and perform ongoing maintenance and optimisation. ### **Incident and Service Management** * Act as an escalation point for the Level 1 Service Desk and wider IT teams. * Take ownership of incidents related to infrastructure and platform services and ensuring timely resolution. * Coordinate with internal teams and managed service providers during incident response. * Assist the Major Incident Manager with root cause analysis for major incidents and contribute to continuous improvement actions. * Maintain accurate documentation of incidents, resolutions, and system changes. ### **General** * Work closely with managed service partners to ensure service performance and availability targets are met, taking proactive steps or escalating to the Service Desk Team Lead where you think targets will be missed. * Contribute to operational improvements, automation, and standardisation across infrastructure services. * Prioritise and manage workloads to effectively meet operational and project deadlines.

title: Infrastructure and Operations – ICT Specialist
description: **Job Title: Infrastructure and Operations – ICT Specialist** **Employer:** Civil Service **County:** Dublin \- Nationwide **Closing Date:** 3pm, Thursday, 21st May 2026 **Starting Salary:** €38,419 **About the Employer:** The Civil Service employs over 50,000 people nationwide across the 18 Government Departments and more than 20 other Civil Service offices and agencies. Our vision is inclusive, high quality and integrated service provision that meets the needs and improves the lives of the people of Ireland. Having the right people in place is essential if we are to deliver on the vision for Ireland to be recognised as a global leader in digital government. In this recruitment competition at Executive Officer (EO) level for Infrastructure and Operations – ICT Specialist we are offering you an exciting chance to be a part of delivering on this vision. Candidates should be aware that the office locations for the vast majority of vacancies likely to arise in Civil Service organisations will be in the wider Dublin area. **About the Role:** As a core part of the digital and ICT function of a Civil Service organisation, the role of Infrastructure and Operations – ICT Specialist is an important one. Infrastructure and Operations Specialists will generally work as part of a multi\-disciplinary digital/ ICT/digital team to design and deliver ICT/digital services for members of the public, businesses and colleagues across their own organisation. The specific duties of each Infrastructure and Operations – ICT Specialist role will largely be determined by the Civil Service organisation to which you are assigned. However, some duties and responsibilities will be common to all Infrastructure and Operations roles such as: • Assisting with administration, implementation, diagnostics, and on\-going support of your organisation’s ICT infrastructure across a broad set of technologies and solutions; • Supporting the management of your organisation’s corporate ICT services to end users such as office productivity applications, email, on\-line collaboration and videoconferencing, access to internet and online services etc; • Assisting management in developing test strategies and test cases and carrying out appropriate testing during the introduction of new solutions; • Supporting end\-to\-end ICT infrastructure and operations solutions across a broad set of leading technologies; • Playing an active role in technical implementation projects and rollout of new hardware, applications and operating systems and ICT/digital services across your organisation which can include developing and providing hands\-on training and demonstrations; **About You:** We are looking for people who are enthusiastic, logical and imaginative, who want to begin their ICT career as part of the Civil Service and are eager to learn and develop new skills. **Do you have:** • strong technical aptitude and see opportunities for digital and ICT to deliver world class government services to citizens and businesses? • good communication skills and are you comfortable working as part of a team or in project groups? **Are you:** • someone who also takes a thorough and structured approach to organising tasks and solving problems and who can deliver on time against project deadlines? • someone who can establish and maintain good working relationships with others, identify their needs and be receptive and responsive to their queries? • confident and persuasive and able to explain complex ideas to a non\-technical audience in a structured and concise manner? • someone who keeps up to date with emerging technologies and is always eager to learn new skills? • happy working in an environment where there is ongoing and significant change? • focused on providing the best possible service to the public and to customers in your organisation? **If so, then we have a role for you!** The Closing Date for this competition is Thursday, 21st of May at 3pm. We are committed to a policy of equal opportunity and encourage applications under all nine grounds of the Employment Equality Act.

title: Junior HRIS Analyst
description: **FOR MORE THAN 80 YEARS,** Natus has been working in collaboration with clinicians to deliver industry\-leading neuro solutions that help providers more easily make sense of the body’s signals. Engineered with input from those who have lived the care experience, our technology simplifies complex processes and improves accuracy and efficiency. There is a role for everyone who wants to be part of the innovative solutions at **Natus Neuro** . **Job Title: Junior HRIS Analyst** **Department:** Human Resources **Location:** Remote in Ireland **Will Report To:** Global HRIS Manager **Role Overview** The Junior HRIS Analyst plays a key role in supporting and optimizing the Dayforce HRIS platform across the organization. This position is responsible for day\-to\-day system support, data integrity, reporting, and configuration, ensuring that HR, Payroll, managers, and employees have a reliable and effective experience when interacting with Dayforce. This role partners closely with HR Services, Payroll, and HRIS leadership to resolve issues, improve system behavior, and support ongoing enhancements. The ideal candidate is detail\-oriented, technically strong, and motivated by improving how HR technology works for its users. **Key Responsibilities** **HRIS Support \& Operations** * Provide Tier 1 support for Dayforce\-related questions and issues * Troubleshoot system issues, identify root causes, and support resolution * Partner with HR Services and Payroll to resolve recurring or high\-impact issues * Escalate complex issues appropriately and support resolution through testing and validation **System Configuration \& Maintenance** * Perform basic Dayforce configuration across modules (e.g., Core HR, Time \& Attendance, Payroll, Talent) * Support regression testing and validation for system changes, patches, and releases * Assist with configuration cleanup and ongoing system maintenance * Maintain documentation and standard operating procedures **Data Integrity \& Compliance** * Monitor and support data integrity across Dayforce * Perform regular audits, validation checks, and compliance testing * Support payroll accuracy through proactive data review and issue identification * Assist with testing related to regulatory or policy changes **Reporting \& Analytics** * Configure and maintain standard and ad hoc Dayforce reports * Partner with HR, Payroll, and leaders to deliver accurate, timely data * Support dashboards and reporting enhancements that improve visibility and decision\-making **Collaboration \& Continuous Improvement** * Partner with HRIS leadership on system enhancements and optimization efforts * Support user acceptance testing (UAT) for new functionality * Contribute to improving system usability and user experience * Help identify opportunities to reduce manual workarounds and recurring issues **What Success Looks Like** * Faster resolution of Dayforce issues and fewer repeat problems * Improved data accuracy and payroll outcomes * Clear, reliable reporting for HR and business stakeholders * A more consistent and predictable Dayforce experience for users **Required Qualifications** * Experience supporting the Dayforce platform * Understanding of HR and payroll processes * Experience with system configuration, testing, and troubleshooting * Strong attention to detail and data accuracy * Ability to work effectively with both technical and non\-technical stakeholders * Ability to travel domestic or internationally 0 \- 15% **Preferred Qualifications** * Hands\-on Dayforce experience across multiple modules * Experience supporting payroll or time \& attendance systems * Experience with HRIS reporting and dashboards * Prior experience in a global or multi\-entity environment **Why Join Us** * Opportunity to play a hands\-on role in improving a global HR platform * Work closely with HR, Payroll, and business partners * Influence how HR technology supports employees and leaders worldwide **Compensation and Benefits** : Along with a competitive salary and bonus structure, we offer a comprehensive benefit package to start: Health \& Life Assurance cover, defined contribution pension plan, active sports and social club, flexible work location, flextime (depending on position), excellent career progression opportunities, free parking, and educational assistance. Be the People part of the Neuro Solution. **Apply Today.** **EEO Statement:** Natus Medical is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, veteran status, disability, sexual orientation, gender identity, or any other protected status.

title: Junior Mechanical Engineer
description: **About LEL** Lynskey Engineering is a mechanical services contractor specialising in the design, installation, and commissioning of HVAC, plumbing, and building services systems across commercial, industrial, and residential projects. The company delivers a wide range of works including new builds, fit\-outs, and refurbishments, with a strong focus on quality, safety, and project coordination, working closely with clients and design teams to ensure efficient and compliant project delivery. Current project portfolio is split between Ireland, UK and mainland Europe. **About the Role** The Jr Mechanical Engineer will be responsible for supporting the Project Management with the delivery of mechanical building services installations on data centre projects across Ireland. Working closely with the project management, design, and site teams, the Jr Mechanical Engineer will ensure that mechanical systems are installed to specification, compliant with local regulations, delivered safely, and meet project quality and commissioning requirements. This is an on\-site role requiring strong technical ability, excellent coordination skills, and the ability to manage stakeholders in a fast\-paced environment. **Our Offer** \- Salary ca. €35,000 to €40,000 pa. \- Location: Dublin initially but occasional requirement for work abroad also. \- Working Hours 42\.5 hours **\- Benefits:** · Employer contribution to pension · Further education support · Performance based annual bonus between 5\-10% · Employee Assistance Programme · Access to free Learning Platform · Bike To Work Scheme **Key Responsibilities** **Technical \& Engineering Support** * Review mechanical drawings, specifications, and submittals to ensure accuracy, coordination, and compliance with project requirements * Assist in developing mechanical design solutions and resolving technical queries * Support procurement by reviewing equipment selections, data sheets, and material approvals * Ensure that all mechanical installations comply with Standards as well as project specifications **Site Coordination \& Installation Oversight** * Monitor and inspect mechanical installations on site, ensuring quality, safety, and compliance with the design * Coordinate with subcontractors, suppliers, and other engineering disciplines to resolve issues and maintain efficient workflow * Attend site meetings, toolbox talks, and coordination workshops as required * Track installation progress and report updates to the Project Manager * Support the development of method statements and risk assessments **Quality Assurance \& Documentation** * Maintain accurate documentation including inspection test plans (ITPs), quality records, and red\-line drawings * Assist with equipment tagging, asset registers, and compliance documentation * Manage technical submittals, RFIs, and design clarifications * Ensure all installation records meet project and client requirements **Testing, Commissioning \& Handover** * Support commissioning teams to ensure systems are tested, balanced, and verified in line with specifications * Assist in preparing O\&M manuals, handover documentation, and as\-built information * Ensure seamless transition from construction to commissioning and handover phases **Health, Safety \& Compliance** * Promote and ensure adherence to site safety standards and project\-specific H\&S requirements. * Liaise with the safety team to address site hazards, unsafe practices, and compliance issues. * Ensure all work follows the project’s Integrated Management System (IMS) and ISO standards **Skills \& Competencies** * Strong technical knowledge of mechanical building services (HVAC, piping, chilled water, heating, ventilation, BMS integration, fire protection systems, etc.) * Excellent technical drawing interpretation and problem\-solving skills * Ability to thrive in a fast\-paced, multinational construction environment * Strong communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to coordinate across multiple teams * Proficient in MS Office and BIM tools (Navisworks, Revit) or similar * Highly organised with a methodical approach to documentation and reporting * Ability to work independently and with team on site . **Experience \& Qualifications** * Bachelor’s degree in mechanical engineering or equivalent qualification * **1\-2 years’** experience in mechanical building services or construction engineering\- * Experience working on large commercial, industrial, data centre, or pharmaceutical projects * Knowledge of Irish and EU construction standards and regulations beneficial

title: Lab IT Administrator (Dublin / Swords)
description: ALGOTEQUE is an IT consultancy firm that helps startups, mid\-sized and large corporations to create and deliver innovative technologies. title: Legal Counsel – Multinational Tech Company
description: My client is looking for an experienced Legal Counsel to join their team in Dublin. They are a major Multinational company growing their team in Ireland at scale. You’ll provide legal advice to management on strategic initiatives, business operations, regulatory matters, and compliance in Ireland. Lead corporate legal activities and ensure timely, effective legal support across the business, including drafting, reviewing, and negotiating contracts. **Key Responsibilities** * Draft, review, and negotiate a wide range of commercial contracts and legal documents. * Advise on contractual, statutory, and regulatory obligations, ensuring ongoing compliance in customer engagements. * Conduct legal due diligence and research to support business decisions. * Provide legal counsel across corporate, commercial, and risk management matters. * Identify and mitigate legal and operational risks. * Interpret statutes, regulations, and contracts, and advise on resolving legal and business issues. * Partner with internal stakeholders to deliver clear, practical legal guidance. * Manage and support legal projects and, where required, supervise junior legal staff. * Report regularly to the Head of Legal on key matters and developments. **Skills \& Experience** * Qualified lawyer (LLB/LLM/JD or equivalent) with a current practising certificate. * 5–10 years’ experience, including at least 5 years in outsourcing, IT contracts, or licensing. * Strong drafting, negotiation, and analytical skills, with solid knowledge of relevant laws. * Effective communicator with the ability to work across all levels and cultures. * Strong organisational, project management, and prioritisation skills. * Collaborative, proactive, and able to operate independently with minimal support. * Experience managing or mentoring team members is an advantage. * Willingness to travel domestically and internationally as required. title: Life Sciences Associate Consultant
description: Are you curious about science, medicine, and pharmaceuticals? Do you consider yourself to be a fast learner, proactive, and diligent? FENIX continues to experience significant growth and is seeking a Life Sciences Associate Consultant to support growing global consulting engagements. Our Life Sciences Associate Consultants leverage their analytic, interpretive, scientific, and communication skills to formulate well\-thought\-out insights that support team decision\-making and execution. FENIX Associate Consultants help derive customer insights through the conduct, management, and analysis of primary and secondary qualitative and quantitative research. They assist in writing concise, accurate summaries of industry and market events using their knowledge and context of the pharmaceutical industry as they contribute to successful project deliveries. In this role, you would have the opportunity to gain consulting experience from a team of FENIX experts, help guide and influence decision\-making for a global pharma company, and develop thought leadership in exciting therapy areas. **RESPONSIBILITIES:** * Conduct comprehensive secondary market research (e.g., monitoring competitor websites, earnings calls, clinical trials, and patient/HCP websites) * Identify and conduct primary market research (e.g., interviews of HCPs/KOLs, industry sources) and provide clear and accurate notes to the team * Assist junior team members with various market\-related tasks * Develop consistent and impactful primary CI * Cover key international conferences (either virtually or in\-person), including key clinical sessions, industry booths, and collect primary research * Develop a comprehensive congress planner * Interpret key clinical trials * Leverage problem\-solving skills and industry research methodologies to derive insights and inform project team and client decision\-making * Conduct quantitative research data analyses * ‪Leverage strong project management and organizational skills to ensure that all tasks are performed in a timely manner and that all client deliverable deadlines are met * Synthesize and communicate results to project teams and clients, leveraging consulting language * Assist in authoring relevant, clear, and succinct client implications, executive summaries, and elevator pitches * ‪Play an active role in deliverables, including client presentations and reports, as well as leveraging tools to strengthen client relations ‪ **QUALIFICATIONS:** * Excellent oral and written communication skills in English (fluent or Level C2 Certificate) **REQUIRED** * EU citizenship or the ability to legally live/work in Dublin, Ireland without the need for sponsorship **REQUIRED** * ‪Minimum of a PhD degree in life sciences (e.g., biochemistry, pharmacology, biology), pharmaceutical business, or related field **REQUIRED** * Life sciences research experience/expertise **REQUIRED** * Good understanding of pharmacology (e.g., pharmacodynamics, routes of administration, receptor biology, signaling pathways) **REQUIRED** * 1\-2 years of experience in the pharmaceutical industry, life sciences research experience, and/or life sciences consulting **PREFERRED** * Attention to detail – Ability to meticulously review deliverables before submitting them for final QC (can you spot errors in your own work?) * Proactivity – at FENIX, we don't simply follow instructions; instead, we critically evaluate whether the instructions are complete or could be applied beyond their initial scope; in addition, providing new ideas on process improvements/efficiencies and methodologies is expected * Resourcefulness – the ability to independently search for an answer and ask for help when needed * Proficiency in the Microsoft Office Suite (Word, PowerPoint, Excel); the ability to craft visual and professional PowerPoint slides from scratch is a big plus * Demonstrated ability to work independently (good work ethic, maturity, and personal initiative), as well as ability to work as a member of a highly collaborative team * ‪Self\-discipline for planning and organizing tasks, ensuring that no task (no matter how minor) is forgotten * The ability to aggregate, organize, distill, and analyze relevant information to derive meaningful implications * Flexibility and ability to adapt to changing project and client needs (while you may initially begin on one project, you may have to do other projects based on changing company needs) * Willingness and ability to travel to domestic and international scientific congresses * Growth mindset, with the ability to work collaboratively as well as autonomously * Ability to think critically and strategically and make assessments keeping the "big picture" in mind, frequently asking yourself: "Does this make sense?" * Humility and ability to take feedback constructively, with a strong desire to learn and grow * The ability to take complex information and make it easy to understand to sometimes non\-scientific audiences

title: Logistics Associate
description: **About The Job** **Who we are:** EOS IT Solutions is a Global Technology and Logistics company, providing Collaboration and Business IT Support services to some of the world’s largest industry leaders, delivering forward\-thinking solutions based on multi\-domain architecture. Customer satisfaction and commitment to superior quality of service are our top business priorities, along with investing in and supporting our partners and employees. Logistics operations team working on the delivery of day\-to\-day operations, deliver excellent service, accuracy and cost KPI’s. * Take a hands\-on approach to work as well as co\-ordinating and completing tasks with the team. * Ensure exceptional performance levels on site across all areas of the operation including, but not restricted to quoting, bookings, tracking, compliance and managing vendors. * Complete all tasks efficiently and effectively. * Assist with cross functional teams to provide the best possible service and cost to our customers. * Lead the team to ensure targets outlined by IT Logistics Business Plans are met, including delivery of KPI’s, values and people processes. * Support the IT Logistics teams to attract, acquire, retain and develop the necessary talent to continuously improve team performance and capability. * Support the preparation and gathering of information any reports or documentation that is to be shared with key stakeholders to help deliver encourage and implement waste reduction initiatives. * Develop a strong team ethos and help create a positive and fun working environment. **What you need to succeed:** * Previous experience working within Logistics is desired. * 2\-4 years’ experience working as part of a team in a challenging environment whilst delivering results. * Experience in IT Logistics \& asset management would be beneficial * Team player, willing to take a hands\-on approach to the role. * Hands\-on strong problem\-solving skills. * Proficiency developing metrics planning (good working knowledge of spreadsheets) EOS is committed to creating a diverse and inclusive work environment and is proud to be an equal\-opportunity employer. We invite you to consider opportunities at EOS regardless of your gender, gender identity, gender reassignment, age, religious or similar philosophical belief, race, national origin, political opinion, sexual orientation, disability, marital or civil partnership status, or other non\-merit factor.

title: Logistics Associate
description: **About \&Open** We’re the global gifting platform for companies that care. Our platform sends gifts globally at scale, while our team guides the process with taste, thought and attention to detail. As a B Corp\-certified company, we’re all about less waste, more impact. At the end of the day, it’s not how much you send, it’s how you make people feel that matters. Our customers include OpenAI, Hubspot, Revolut and Intercom. Come and grow with us. **About The Team** Our Logistics team is growing! We’re obsessed with enabling the delivery of gifts to people across the globe, and work to create the best possible solutions for our customers and the people they wish to gift. To make this all happen, we collaborate closely to understand each client's needs and find the most effective methods to surprise and delight the lucky gift recipients. We care deeply about the journey of our gifts, carefully planning every step from source to doorstep. **About The Role** Come and join our logistics team to support fulfilment operations in our Dublin warehouse. This is an active role that combines hands\-on warehouse work — sorting, packing and managing stock — with system\-side responsibilities such as monitoring our WMS, coordinating delivery exceptions and supporting wider operational initiatives. **What you'll do?** * Pick, pack and dispatch outbound shipments accurately, across a range of sizes, formats and gift types. * Receive, check and book inbound shipments into the Warehouse Management System (WMS), ensuring accuracy and timely stock availability. * Work as a team to ensure all orders are shipped within the SLA and contribute towards the successful achievement of targets. * Manage the flow of goods through our Dublin studio warehouse. * Support the planning and execution of large operational initiatives such as stock moves, warehouse changes and ongoing process improvements across the logistics function. * Monitor and analyse in orders across our fulfilment partners to identify discrepancies, delays and fulfilment risks. * Coordinate delivery exception for shipments in transit by identifying issues and supporting investigations and solutions. * Perform system and administrative updates to ensure data accuracy across operational tools. * Assist with other ad\-hoc tasks required to support the day\-to\-day running of Logistics operations. * Support the wider Logistics team by providing cover during holidays, busy periods and team absences. **What You'll Need** * Fluent written and spoken English is essential. * Experience of working in a customer focused environment. * 1–3 years of experience in a warehouse, fulfilment, or logistics environment. * Comfortable with the physical demands of the role: standing for extended periods, lifting and moving boxes, and working in an active warehouse environment. * Experience working with a WMS or similar inventory/order management system is a strong plus. * Exceptional attention to detail, a positive can\-do attitude, time management, and organisational skills. * Ability to work in a fast\-paced environment to tight timelines. **Benefits** * Competitive Salary * Company Pension * Healthcare cover * 25 days Annual Leave \+ 2 Company Days * Maternity and Paternity Paid Leave * Exciting experience in a fast\-paced start\-up environment * Employee Stock Options Plan **Location** Head Office: Dublin, Harold's Cross **All Welcome Here Statement** We believe diverse teammates, opinions and backgrounds generate a larger global impact and more considerate acts of care. \&Open is an equal opportunity employer and we do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, colour, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status or disability status.

title: MERCHANDISER - ROI
description: LEARN MORE ABOUT US AND THE ROLE:**Location:** Ballybrack (A96 CD72, D18 F5F7\) **Hours:** 5\.5 hours per week **Days:** Monday \& Friday **Benefits:** Holiday Entitlement, Merchandiser Recognition Programme, Employee Benefits Scheme, Expenses/Mileage/Travel Time where applicable As an inclusive employer Carlton Greetings are passionate about creating a workforce that represents the diversity of our communities and the customers we supply.
